hook.text: The English word father, Latin pater, and Sanskrit pitar all come from one ancient root. Sound shifts left clues.

buildup.text: Historical linguistics traces how languages change over time using regular sound shifts and word comparisons.

discovery.text: Grimm's Law explained systematic consonant shifts from Proto-Indo-European to Germanic languages — like p to f.

twist.text: Languages change constantly but so slowly that speakers rarely notice. Each generation thinks its speech is stable.

climax.text: Historical linguistics reveals that every language alive today is a slow-motion transformation of something older.

punchline.text: Every word carries the ghost of its ancestor.

quiz.question: What does Grimm's Law describe?
options: Systematic consonant shifts between language families
